prompt:
A KnowledgeOps Content & Data Standardization management system that includes offline APIs for managing publishable knowledge items, reusable structured templates, and governed data operations for internal teams. The backend, built with Actix-web and Diesel on PostgreSQL, exposes Authentication APIs for username/password login with role-based access (Administrator, Author, Reviewer, Analyst) and session expiration after 12 hours of inactivity. Template & Form APIs support template-based creation of knowledge items with configurable fields (string/number/date/enum/text), field-level constraints (required, max length 2,000 characters for free text, regex for IDs, enum option sets), and cross-field validation (e.g., score must be 0–100 when “graded” is true). Version Management APIs maintain immutable version history for templates and items; publishing always targets a specific version, rollback is allowed to the last 10 versions, and every item receives an auto-number formatted as KO-YYYYMMDD-##### with a daily counter reset. Status Transition APIs enforce Draft → In Review → Approved → Published → Archived, where only Reviewers can approve, and an item auto-reverts to Draft if In Review is idle for 14 days. Search & Retrieval APIs provide full-text search over title/body/tags with relevance and newest sorting, filters by channel/tag/time window, query suggestions derived from the last 30 days, trending terms computed daily, and a per-user search history capped at 200 entries with a clear operation. Bulk Import/Export APIs accept Excel/CSV based on downloadable templates and run pre-import checks for required fields, formats, duplicates (by auto-number or normalized title+channel within 90 days), option validity, and scoring rules; results include row-level error reports, allow partial success, and exports can conditionally include explanations with optional masking of sensitive strings.

Data model requirements include tables for users, roles, sessions, templates, template_versions, items, item_versions, tags, channels, audits, searches, imports, exports, metrics_snapshots, events, and feature_flags; primary keys are UUIDs, created_at/updated_at are required, and key indexes include full-text GIN on item title/body, btree on auto-number, status, channel_id, and published_at. The data cleansing and standardization pipeline runs fully locally as asynchronous jobs and produces versioned standardized models via schema mappings: deduplication uses deterministic fingerprints (normalized text + key fields), missing values can be imputed only with explicit defaults (never inferred personally identifying fields), units and time zones normalize to US customary units and America/New_York timestamps while preserving original raw values, and outliers are flagged using simple z-score thresholds (|z| ≥ 3) for analyst review. Security and compliance require strict input validation, SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries, API rate limiting at 60 requests/minute/user with a local CAPTCHA challenge after repeated failed logins (no external services), upload whitelisting to CSV/XLSX only with size cap 10 MB and basic file signature checks, audit logs for all create/update/status changes retained 7 years, field-level masking for exports (e.g., redact emails/phone patterns), and encrypted at-rest storage for passwords (Argon2id) and sensitive columns. Monitoring and analytics include health-check capability, slow-query logging (over 500 ms), error-rate tracking, and exception alerts written to local logs and an on-disk “alert queue” (no email/webhooks); KPI and operational analytics APIs support custom filters, scheduled snapshot generation, data export, event instrumentation, and optional A/B feature toggles stored locally, all deployable as a single Dockerized service without network dependencies.
